name: Phase 3 CRM intake and opportunity-to-project requirements matrix

packages/crm-core/src/intakePrimitives.test.ts covers tenant-owned primitive creation without Bitrix/AmoCRM coupling, cross-tenant reference rejection without leaking referenced details, stable key/date/probability validation, defensive copies, malformed runtime arrays/metadata, and missing or unvalidated stage refs

evidence: packages/tenant-config/src/index.ts defines tenant-owned OpportunityCategoryDefinition, OpportunityTypologyDefinition, OpportunityIntakeTemplate, OpportunityIntakeMetadataRegistry, and OpportunityIntakeRequirements with stable system keys, active flags, versioning, labels, and deterministic trace output

createOpportunityIntakeMetadataRegistry validates tenant ownership, duplicate category/typology/custom-field/template keys, opportunity-only custom-field targets, known category/typology references, active custom-field requirements, and finite Phase 3 standard intake field keys
resolveOpportunityIntakeRequirements returns category/typology labels and required standard/custom intake fields from tenant configuration/templates, not from crm-core hardcoded company terms

evidence: packages/resource-planning/src/index.ts defines DemandTemplateProfile, DemandTemplateRoleRule, DemandFormulaMetadata, DemandEstimate, StageRoleDemand, and ResourcePlanningModelError for deterministic Phase 3 demand estimation

estimateDemandFromTemplateMatch produces sorted stage/role planned work, total planned work, scenario label, formula key/version/label, per-role confidence, source assumptions, top-level template-match assumptions, and trace output from a matched template plus opportunity scope hints

Demand formula uses finite deterministic baseWorkHours plus numeric scopeHintDriver hoursPerUnit inputs; nonnumeric driven scope hints, unmatched template results, tenant mismatches, and template/profile version drift are rejected with typed errors

Receiving Code Review fixed an Important template-match provenance gap: DemandTemplateMatchInput now carries tenantId and opportunityId, and estimateDemandFromTemplateMatch rejects template-match tenant/opportunity mismatches before using match assumptions

Second Receiving Code Review fixed an Important source-data provenance gap: DemandScopeHint is now tenant/opportunity-owned and scope-hint tenant/opportunity mismatches are rejected before formula evaluation
packages/resource-planning/package.json and package-lock.json record @kiss-pm/domain-core as a workspace dependency
Bug Hunt found and fixed silent demand undercount risks: missing driven scope hints and negative numeric scope hints now fail with typed validation errors instead of producing misleading planned work
Bug Hunt after review fix added a top-level demandEstimate object guard so malformed callers receive ResourcePlanningModelError instead of a TypeError
Final Receiving Code Review fixed an Important formula overflow gap: derived driver contribution, role planned work, and total planned work are now finite-checked before returning an estimate
Final Requesting Code Review pass returned no remaining Critical/Important findings for P3-005

evidence: packages/resource-planning/src/index.ts defines deterministic CapacityDateWindow, RoleCapacityBucket, ResourceReservation, RoleCapacityFeasibility, CapacityFeasibilityBlocker, and CapacityFeasibilityResult contracts for Phase 3 seeded capacity feasibility

assessCapacityFeasibility compares a tenant/opportunity demand estimate against seeded role capacity buckets and active conflicting reservations for the expected date window, returning fit/overloaded status, severity, role gaps, blockers, conflicting reservations, assumptions, and trace

packages/resource-planning/src/capacityFeasibility.test.ts covers fit, overload, seeded conflict, cross-tenant demand/capacity/reservation rejection, malformed input, and impossible calendar date rejection
packages/resource-planning/src/capacityFeasibility.integration.test.ts composes P3-005 demand estimation with P3-006 capacity feasibility against seeded capacity/reservation fixtures

Bug Hunt found and fixed two Important validation gaps: all capacity buckets/reservations are tenant-checked before date/status filtering, and impossible YYYY-MM-DD calendar dates are rejected instead of relying on JavaScript Date normalization

Receiving Code Review found and fixed an Important partial-window calculation gap: Phase 3 now rejects overlapping capacity buckets/reservations that are not fully inside the assessed expected window instead of counting full-period hours

Second Receiving Code Review found and fixed an Important duplicate identity gap: capacity bucket and reservation ids must be unique before feasibility math so seeded data cannot double-count capacity or conflicts

Final Receiving Code Review found and fixed two Important gaps: feasibility now rejects inconsistent demand totals that do not match stage-role demand rows, and tenant validation runs before duplicate-id analysis to avoid leaking foreign capacity/reservation ids
Last Receiving Code Review fixed the remaining tenant-isolation ordering gap: malformed foreign capacity/reservation records are tenant-checked before deep structural date validation
Final Requesting Code Review pass returned no remaining Critical/Important findings for P3-006

evidence: apps/api/src/app.ts exposes Phase 3 CRM intake routes for account/contact/opportunity create/read, readiness, template match, feasibility, and a permission-checked project-draft command entrypoint that returns safe 501 not_implemented until P3-008

apps/api/src/app.ts validates CRM create and command payloads with strict Zod schemas, rejects unknown external CRM payload fields, rejects public client-provided ids, and returns stable DTOs without Bitrix/AmoCRM adapter fields

apps/api/src/phase3Runtime.ts provides seeded in-memory Phase 3 CRM runtime state wired to crm-core/project-core/resource-planning domain functions for readiness, template matching, demand estimation, and capacity feasibility
apps/api/src/phase2Runtime.ts adds CRM intake and project-draft permissions to the Phase 2 access catalog and tenant-scoped fixture profiles so API routes enforce permissions server-side

apps/api/src/phase3CrmApi.test.ts covers account/contact/opportunity create/read, existing account/contact linking, tenant-scoped listing, readiness/template-match/feasibility responses, permission denial, cross-tenant hidden reads, safe foreign-reference validation, strict payload validation, and the P3-008 project-draft boundary
Bug Hunt found and fixed missing account/contact CRUD coverage plus command-body/external-field validation gaps before full gate

Requesting Code Review found two Important issues: client-provided ids leaked cross-tenant existence through conflicts, and opportunity create could not link existing account/contact records; Receiving Code Review fixed both by rejecting public ids, generating tenant-scoped ids, adding accountId/contactIds references, tenant-validating references, and adding regression tests
Second Requesting Code Review pass returned no remaining Critical/Important findings for the reviewed P3-007 CRM create/linking paths

rows.requirement: Project draft creation from qualified opportunity
evidence: packages/project-core/src/index.ts defines ProjectDraft with tenant-owned source opportunity snapshot, process template snapshot, demand snapshot, feasibility snapshot, status draft, actor, createdAt, and correlationId
packages/project-core/src/projectDraft.test.ts covers tenant-owned project draft creation from an opportunity and cross-tenant source snapshot rejection

packages/action-engine/src/index.ts implements executeCreateProjectDraftFromOpportunity as a governed command with required permission project_draft.create, readiness and feasibility preconditions, duplicate draft conflict detection, action execution source/target refs, before/after summary, actor, timestamp, trace, and correlationId

packages/action-engine/src/projectDraftCommand.test.ts covers successful command execution, before/after action evidence, immutable processTemplate snapshot with assumptions, readiness precondition failure, and overloaded feasibility precondition failure

apps/api/src/phase3Runtime.ts wires P3-007 readiness/template/demand/feasibility into P3-008 project draft command execution and stores cloned project draft/action execution results in the Phase 3 runtime

apps/api/src/app.ts changes POST /api/crm/opportunities/:opportunityId/project-draft from the P3-007 501 boundary to a 201 governed command response, adds GET /api/projects/:id, adds GET /api/audit?targetType=&targetId= filtered readback, and records audit target as the source opportunity while actionExecution.target remains the created projectDraft
apps/api/src/phase2Runtime.ts adds project_draft.read and preserves project_draft.create permissions for tenant-scoped profiles

apps/api/src/phase3ProjectDraftApi.test.ts covers authorized project draft creation, project readback, targeted opportunity audit readback with template assumptions, unauthorized denial, cross-tenant hidden denial, duplicate draft conflict, and readiness precondition failure

Bug Hunt found and fixed an Important audit traceability gap: audit readback now supports /api/audit filters and stores the source opportunity as the audit target instead of only the created draft target

Requesting Code Review found one Important gap: action/audit evidence did not durably record process template assumptions; Receiving Code Review fixed actionExecution.after.processTemplate to include templateId, key, version, matchConfidence, and assumptions, with command and API audit assertions
Second Requesting Code Review pass returned no remaining Critical/Important findings for the reviewed P3-008 project draft creation path

rows.requirement: CRM Intake Control web surface and E2E suite
evidence: apps/web/src/crmIntakeApiClient.ts adds typed Phase 3 browser client methods for opportunity list/create, readiness, feasibility, project draft command/readback, and opportunity audit evidence using the /api/api Vite proxy base path required by server /api routes

apps/web/src/CrmIntakeControlSurface.tsx adds a Russian guided CRM Intake Control surface with opportunity form/list/card, readiness blockers, feasibility demand/capacity summaries, project-draft action, permission-aware controls, API denial feedback, reload draft readback, and action/audit evidence display
apps/web/src/App.tsx wires CRM Intake Control into the authenticated shell while preserving Phase 2 admin surface tests and fixture auth behavior
apps/api/src/phase2Runtime.ts grants project_manager audit.read so the project draft control surface can show governed action evidence after creation
e2e/tests/phase3/*.spec.ts implements E2E-020..024 with UI actions, direct API/domain readback, reload persistence, unauthorized project-draft denial, and Tenant B hidden-data checks
docs/e2e/E2E_SCENARIOS.md marks E2E-020..024 passing after the exact Phase 3 Playwright command passed

Bug Hunt found one High audit-integrity issue: the UI briefly displayed command-response fallback as audit evidence when /api/audit readback failed or returned empty; Receiving Code Review fixed this by rendering only persisted audit readback in the audit list and showing an explicit not-confirmed state otherwise

Bug Hunt also found a Medium stale-audit race from global auditEvents state; Receiving Code Review fixed audit state by opportunityId so async readbacks cannot paint one opportunity audit under another selected card
Follow-up Bug Hunt and Requesting Code Review returned no remaining Critical/High/Important findings for the P3-009 audit fixes and current working tree
